examples: Wajdi Zeinaddin of the Al-Wafd nationalist daily urged the Muslim Brotherhood to step down.
Ala Oreibi of Al-Wafd warned the Brotherhood that winning an election was no guarantee of staying in power.
Fouad Serageddin, the octogenarian boss of the conservative New Wafd Party, served as interior minister in King Farouk's last government before the 1952 revolution.
